title: Carbonate ion
summary: A polyatomic ion: one carbon and three oxygens carrying a 2- charge as a single unit. It stays intact through a reaction — you carry $\mathrm{CO_3^{2-}}$ around whole, never as loose C and O. Its molar mass sums the four atoms; the tiny mass of the two extra electrons is conventionally ignored.
notes: The 2- charge is the sourced ionic charge (see the Valence Table); the composition and molar mass here are the machine-checked part.
